2603.07709.title: YAQIN: Culturally Sensitive, Agentic AI for Mental Healthcare Support Among Muslim Women in the UK
2603.07709.paper_summary:
Mental healthcare services in the UK lack tools and resources to address the cultural needs of Muslim women, often leaving them feeling as though their values are pathologised and limiting trust and engagement [1]. Despite growing awareness of cultural competency, few interventions integrate Islamic frameworks into therapeutic support. This report investigates the design and evaluation of YAQIN, a co-designed AI-based application supporting culturally and faith-sensitive mental health engagement for Muslim women. With almost 1.9 million Muslim women in England in 2021, YAQIN responds to a gap in care [2]. It leverages AIś anonymity and continuous support through a faith-aware chatbot and guided journaling tool grounded in user-centred design and Islamic psychology.
  The YAQIN design research methodology comprised three stages: contextual investigation and literature review, user research with N=14 stakeholders including Muslim women and mental health experts, and prototype development informed by deductive thematic analysis, personas, journey maps, and design specifications.
  Evaluation involved a co-designed user study with five participants: four Muslim women and one mental health expert who reviewed therapeutic alignment and cultural sensitivity after using the chatbot prototype. Feedback focused on tone, faith relevance, emotional resonance, and the Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline enabling contextual continuity.
  Participants highlighted YAQINś ability to bridge cultural gaps in trust and therapeutic confidence. Feedback included suggestions of including linguistic diversity and routine-based guidance.
  This project demonstrates how culturally sensitive AI can improve mental healthcare accessibility and trust for marginalised communities and highlights the potential of faith-integrated technology in healthcare innovation.
2603.07709.paper_summary_zh:
英國的心理健康服務缺乏工具和資源來滿足穆斯林女性的文化需求，常常讓她們感到自己的價值觀被病理化，從而限制了信任和參與 [1]。儘管對文化能力的認識日益增長，但很少有干預措施將伊斯蘭框架整合到治療支持中。本報告調查了YAQIN的設計和評估，這是一個共同設計的基於AI的應用程序，旨在支持穆斯林女性的文化和信仰敏感的心理健康參與。2021年英格蘭有近190萬穆斯林女性，YAQIN響應了護理中的一個空白 [2]。它利用AI的匿名性和通過一個信仰意識的聊天機器人和以用戶為中心設計和伊斯蘭心理學為基礎的引導日記工具提供持續支持。

YAQIN的設計研究方法論包括三個階段：背景調查和文獻回顧、與N=14名利益相關者（包括穆斯林女性和心理健康專家）的用戶研究，以及基於演繹性主題分析、角色、旅程地圖和設計規範的信息的原型開發。

評估涉及一項共同設計的用戶研究，參與者包括五位參與者：四名穆斯林女性和一名心理健康專家，在使用聊天機器人原型後檢視治療的一致性和文化敏感性。反饋集中在語氣、信仰相關性、情感共鳴以及促進上下文連貫性的檢索增強生成管道上。

參與者強調了YAQIN在建立信任和治療信心方面彌合文化差距的能力。反饋包括建議增加語言多樣性和基於常規的指導。

該項目展示了文化敏感的AI如何改善邊緣社區的心理健康服務可及性和信任，並突顯了信仰整合技術在醫療創新中的潛力。
2603.07709.author: Yasmin Zaraket et.al.
